title: 59-subject 40-class SSVEP dataset
description: A comprehensive steady-state visually evoked potential (SSVEP) dataset comprising EEG recordings from 59 healthy subjects (mean age 12.4 years) performing a 40-class visual speller task. The dataset includes 8-channel EEG data sampled at 250 Hz with stimulation frequencies ranging from 8.0 to 15.8 Hz at 0.2 Hz resolution, designed to support brain-computer interface research and benchmarking of SSVEP classification algorithms.
methods_description: EEG data were acquired using a NeuSenW system (Neuracle) with 8 semi-dry pre-gelled electrodes positioned at POz, PO3, PO4, PO7, PO8, Oz, O1, and O2 according to the standard 1005 montage. Reference and ground electrodes were placed at Fp1 and Fp2 respectively. Sampling rate was 250 Hz with a 50 Hz line frequency. Subjects performed a SSVEP speller task with 40 visual flicker stimuli at distinct frequencies, with each trial lasting 4 seconds and a trial interval of 0.5-4.5 seconds. Four blocks of 160 trials total were collected per subject in a single session.
